div.head: The Consul at Geneva (Gilbert) to the Secretary of State
div.p:
186. Department’s 70, June 20, 11 a.m.Telegram No. 70 instructed the Consul to “telegraph substance of replies as received.” (724.3415/3841) The following is a paraphrase of the British reply:
“Government propose to continue for the present to hold up the issuing of licenses’ for the export of arms to these two countries but feel it would be inadvisable for them to make a formal declaration on the lines proposed in the Committee’s report until the governments of all the countries included in the list of countries whose cooperation Great Britain regards as essential (see Consulate’s 98, May 28 noon) have announced that they are willing to participate without reservations.”For slightly different text, see League of Nations, Official Journal, July 1934, p. 842.
